message:
fix(pager): prevent db_size regression in WAL group commit waiters

Root cause of persistent WAL benchmark BusySnapshot failure:

After group commit, the flusher correctly sets inner.db_size to the
consolidated max across all batched transactions. However, waiters
were then REVERTING db_size to their own local committed_db_size value
in the post-commit code, potentially shrinking db_size below the actual
database size.

This caused a cascade of failures:
1. Waiter's committed_db_size could be lower than flusher's final_db_size
2. After waiter's post-commit, db_size would regress (e.g., 10 → 6)
3. Pages 7-10 still exist in WAL but db_size says only 6 pages
4. Next transaction reads page 7 → page doesn't exist in db file
5. Snapshot detects inconsistency → BusySnapshot error

Fix: In WAL mode, skip the db_size update in post-commit since the
flusher already set the correct consolidated value. Journal mode still
needs the update since it doesn't use the group commit path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
